name: claw-shot
description: Screenshots straight into Claude Code. In-app capture shortcuts plus a watch mode that auto-injects native Cmd+Shift+3/4 screenshots from any app. No drag-drop.

commands.title: Capture Full Screen into Claude Code
Toggle Watch Mode (auto-inject screenshots from any app)

clawShot.watchOnStartup.description: Start in watch mode: auto-inject any screenshot saved to the macOS screenshot folder (native Cmd+Shift+3/4), from any app, even while VS Code is in the background. Toggle anytime with Cmd+Option+0 or the status-bar button.

clawShot.screenshotDirectory.description: Folder to watch for screenshots in watch mode. Leave empty to auto-detect the macOS screenshot location (defaults to ~/Desktop). Supports ~.

clawShot.saveDirectory.description: Directory for in-app (Cmd+Option) captures. Leave empty to use the OS temp directory. Supports ~.

clawShot.targetTerminalName.description: Only send to a terminal whose name contains this string (case-insensitive). Leave empty to use the active terminal. Tip: rename your Claude terminal to 'claude' and set this to 'claude'.

clawShot.focusTerminalAfterCapture.description: Reveal and focus the target terminal after an in-app capture so you can type your prompt immediately.

clawShot.keepFiles.description: Keep in-app capture files. When false, Claw Shot's own files older than 24h are pruned on startup. (Watch mode never deletes your native screenshots.)

clawShot.playSound.description: Play the macOS screenshot shutter sound on in-app captures.
